_note: This is a filled example. Copy brand-config.json from schemas/, fill it in, save it in your working directory.

brand.name: MillionaireCopy
brand.one_line: Helps early-stage founders turn AI + content into predictable demand — without becoming a full-time influencer

voice.tone: calm, analytical, zero hype — writes like a founder texting a peer after noticing a pattern
voice.pov: pattern recognition — studies thousands of success stories and filters out what actually works
voice.anti: no guru language, no hype, no sensationalism, not chasing virality — chasing buyers
voice.reader_position: reader is always the hero — brand is the shortcut, not the star

audience.who: early-stage SaaS founders, service business founders, solopreneurs building with AI
pain: I don't know what to post
I don't want to become a cringe influencer
I want customers not vanity metrics

pillars: AI for Founders — workflows, tools, practical use (non-technical)
Founder-Led Content Systems — ideas to posts to leads
Demand and Revenue — why some content converts, others don't
Case Studies and Reverse Engineering — lessons from $1M+ founders
Personal Operator Narrative — contrarian takes, building in public

writing_rules.second_line: must recook the hook — add a twist or deepen the intrigue
writing_rules.paragraph_style: grouped — 2-3 related sentences together, never single lines stacked
writing_rules.closer: always a question or sharp one-liner
writing_rules.dash: regular dash - only, never em dash

platform_tone.linkedin: founder-to-founder, analytical, calm authority — peer sharing a pattern they noticed
platform_tone.x: punchy, reactive, own take in real time — thought mid-build not a news summary
platform_tone.instagram: same as LinkedIn but slightly warmer, more visual language, help the reader picture it
platform_tone.facebook: personal, conversational, like a message to a smart friend — opens with observation, shares data mid-story, no bullet lists

image_prompt.background: light grid or off-white paper — no coffee stains, not always cream
image_prompt.headline: hand-lettered, bold, states the achievement or core insight
image_prompt.chart: one required — bar, pie, timeline, or flow — hand-drawn style
image_prompt.icons: hand-drawn for any tools, concepts, or steps mentioned
image_prompt.colours: multi-colour ink — navy, rust, teal, amber, yellow accents
image_prompt.feel: smart founder's notebook — not Canva, not corporate, not sketch
image_prompt.rule: image alone must tell the full story without reading the post

training_posts.post:
Her company was worth $3M yesterday.
Her personal brand is worth $30M today.

Yesterday Diarrha Ndiaye announced her $3M beauty brand was closing. Within an hour she had coordinated media coverage across every major outlet.

She turned her business failure into a media tour.

1. Planned the closure like a product launch — press releases, exclusive interviews, her own narrative, all within hours
2. Made herself the protagonist, not the victim
3. Used data as armor — 'Black founders get less than 1% of VC funding'
4. Positioned her failure as market research

Your company's value is temporary.
Your personal brand's value is permanent.

What failure are you hiding that could become your expertise?
training_posts.why_it_worked: Unexpected contrast hook + numbered framework + reframe + closing question. Specific story, transferable lesson.

training_posts.post:
Anthropic added $6B in one month.
Not one year. One month.

End of 2025: $9B ARR.
End of February 2026: $19B.

That is not growth. That is a different category of business.

Most SaaS companies take a decade to hit $1B ARR.
Anthropic added 6x that in 28 days.

What this means if you are building right now:
the platform you are building on is growing faster than anyone predicted.
The window is still open.

What are you building on top of it?
training_posts.why_it_worked: Real verified number as hook. Short punchy sentences. Builder framing not news framing. Ends with engagement question.

training_posts.post:
You don't need millions. You need margin.

I'd rather have a business doing $10K/month at 90% profit with 100% ownership than make $100K/month pumping most of it back into paid ads.

Freedom over scale.

What would you prefer?
training_posts.why_it_worked: Reframe opener. Specific comparison with real numbers. One-word value statement. Short engagement question.
